manifest.name: Accordion
manifest.description: An accordion component for displaying a list of collapsible items, revealing content when expanded.

minimal.title: Basic
minimal.code: <Accordion.Root type="single" collapsible><Accordion.Item value="item-1"><Accordion.Header><Accordion.Trigger>Is it accessible?</Accordion.Trigger></Accordion.Header><Accordion.Content>Yes. It adheres to WAI-ARIA design pattern.</Accordion.Content></Accordion.Item></Accordion.Root>

common.title: Example 2
common.code: <Accordion.Root type="multiple"><Accordion.Item value="item-1"><Accordion.Header><Accordion.Trigger>What is Radix UI?</Accordion.Trigger></Accordion.Header><Accordion.Content>Radix UI is an open-source component library for building high-quality, accessible design systems and web apps.</Accordion.Content></Accordion.Item><Accordion.Item value="item-2"><Accordion.Header><Accordion.Trigger>How do I install it?</Accordion.Trigger></Accordion.Header><Accordion.Content>You can install Radix UI components using npm or yarn.</Accordion.Content></Accordion.Item></Accordion.Root>

common.title: Example 3
common.code: import { ChevronDownIcon } from '@radix-ui/react-icons'; Custom Trigger Icon This accordion item uses a custom icon for the trigger.

common.title: With click handler

guidance.whenToUse: To present a large amount of information in a compact, organized manner, such as FAQs, categorized product details, or expandable sections within a form. It helps reduce visual clutter and improves information scanning.
guidance.whenNotToUse: Avoid using for critical information that requires immediate visibility or for content that users need to compare across sections simultaneously. Use tabs or a multi-column layout instead for comparative content.
guidance.accessibility: The Accordion component is built on Radix UI primitives, ensuring strong accessibility out-of-the-box. It includes proper ARIA attributes (`aria-expanded`, `aria-controls`), keyboard navigation (Tab to navigate between headers, Space/Enter to toggle expansion), and focus management. Ensure that content within Accordion.Content is perceivable and operable by all users.

manifest.semanticDescription: The Accordion component provides a robust and accessible way to display a list of collapsible items, where each item can reveal or hide its associated content. Built on Radix UI, it offers excellent keyboard navigation and ARIA support, making it highly usable. Key features include the ability to control whether single or multiple items can be expanded at once, customizable triggers, and smooth transitions. It is commonly used for frequently asked questions (FAQs), organizing lengthy content sections, navigation menus with nested items, or filtering options in a constrained space. The Accordion helps reduce visual clutter and improves information scanning by allowing users to focus on relevant sections.
